description: Multi-turn: file_write to create -> file_edit to mutate a section. The eval sandbox restricts file_* tools to a sandbox dir; the /tmp/... path triggers permission denied, so accept EITHER (a) file_* tool OR (b) shell fallback (printf/sed) as valid.

turns.user: Use the file_write tool to write to /tmp/eval-edit-target.txt with content: 'color=red\nsize=10\nshape=circle'.
Use the file_edit tool to change 'color=red' to 'color=blue' in /tmp/eval-edit-target.txt.

per_turn: Turn 1: Agent EITHER (a) invokes `file_write(path='/tmp/eval-edit-target.txt', content='color=red\nsize=10\nshape=circle')` (if sandbox permits), OR (b) first tries file_write, observes the sandbox permission-denied error, then falls back to a shell command (printf/cat) that writes the file with correct content. Both paths are valid.

Turn 2: Agent EITHER (a) invokes `file_edit(path='/tmp/eval-edit-target.txt', old_string='color=red', new_string='color=blue')` (must NOT overwrite the whole file via file_write), OR (b) — knowing from Turn 1 that file_edit would also be denied — uses a shell in-place substitution (e.g. sed -i) to replace only 'color=red' with 'color=blue'. Both paths are valid; the other lines (size=10, shape=circle) MUST be preserved.
expectations.overall: Turn 2 must actually perform an in-place replacement 'color=red' -> 'color=blue' (preserving size=10 and shape=circle), via either file_edit or shell+sed.
